# A Short Position Paper on Reproducibility

## Abstract

We argue that reproducibility standards in ML should be treated as a
first-class submission requirement, not an afterthought.

## Background

The machine learning community has made progress on artifact evaluation
but lacks uniform standards across venues.

## Position

Reviewers should be empowered to reject papers whose central claims cannot
be reproduced from the provided artifacts.

## Conclusion

We call on program chairs to adopt a reproducibility checklist as a hard
gate for submission.
